tools.name: inventory.getInventory
tools.description: You can use this API to get the inventory for a given item.

tools.name: inventory.getMultiNodeInventoryForAllSkuAndAllShipNodes
tools.description: This API will retrieve the inventory count for all of a seller's items across all ship nodes by item to ship node mapping. Inventory can be zero or non-zero. Please note that NextCursor value changes and it needs to be passed on from the previous call to next call.

tools.name: inventory.getMultiNodeInventoryForSkuAndAllShipnodes
tools.description: This API will retrieve the inventory count for an item across all ship nodes or one specific ship node. You can specify the ship node for which you want to fetch the inventory

tools.name: inventory.getWfsInventory
tools.description: You can use this API to get the current Available to Sell inventory quantities for all WFS items in your catalog. You can also query specific SKUs or filter to only items updated after a specific date in order to reduce the response size.

tools.name: inventory.updateBulkInventory
tools.description: Updates inventory for items in bulk. Seller Can either use feed type "inventory" or "MP_INVENTORY" * Inventory spec 1.4 feed type: inventory * Inventory spec 1.5 feed type: MP_INVENTORY Please Note: Multi Node Inventory Update Feed (feedType=MP_INVENTORY) only supports JSON Request and Responses. Refer to "MultiNode_Bulk_Inventory_Update_Request.json" for the corresponding request sample Refer to the guide section for more detailed guide around each of the feed types Refer to the throttling limits before uploading the Feed Files.

tools.name: inventory.updateInventoryForAnItem
tools.description: Updates the inventory for a given item.

tools.name: inventory.updateMultiNodeInventory
tools.description: This API will update the inventory for an item across one or more fulfillment centers, known as ship nodes.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
